words.definition: An instrument for measuring atmospheric pressure.
words.example: The falling barometer warned of a storm.
The hygrometer confirmed the room was too dry.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring wind speed.
words.example: The anemometer spun rapidly in the gale.
The altimeter showed the plane at 30,000 feet.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring distance travelled.

words.example: The speedometer needle crept past ninety.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring rotational speed.
An instrument for measuring electric current.
words.example: The ammeter registered a small leak.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring electric voltage.
words.example: The voltmeter showed a steady 12 volts.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring electrical resistance.
words.example: The ohmmeter identified the broken conductor.

words.definition: An instrument that combines voltmeter, ammeter, and ohmmeter.
words.example: A multimeter is essential to any electrician.

words.definition: An instrument for detecting small electric currents.
words.example: The galvanometer deflected at the slightest current.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring the pressure of gases or liquids.
An instrument for measuring liquid pressure at depth.
An instrument for measuring blood pressure.
An instrument for listening to internal sounds of the body.
words.example: The stethoscope revealed a faint murmur.

words.definition: An instrument for examining the ear.
words.example: The otoscope showed a mild infection.

words.definition: An instrument for examining the interior of the eye.
An instrument for viewing very small objects.
An instrument for viewing distant objects.
words.example: The telescope resolved the ring system.

words.definition: An instrument for observing over an obstacle.
A precise timepiece, especially one used at sea.
words.example: The chronometer solved the longitude problem.

words.definition: A device that marks time by regular beats.
words.example: The metronome kept the pianist steady.
The seismograph traced the tremor faithfully.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring sea depth.
A system for detecting objects underwater by sound.
A system for detecting objects by radio waves.
A detection system that uses laser light.
A surveying instrument for measuring angles.
words.example: The theodolite fixed the exact boundary.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring the angle between two visible objects, especially for navigation.
words.example: The sextant remains a backup for celestial navigation.

words.definition: A historical instrument for observing celestial positions.

words.example: The compass needle swung to magnetic north.
The protractor helped mark a perfect 45 degrees.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring the dimensions of an object.
An instrument for measuring very small distances or thicknesses.
words.example: The micrometer measured the shim to a thousandth of an inch.

words.definition: A device using a sliding secondary scale for fine measurement.
An instrument for measuring optical density.
words.example: Radiographers use a densitometer to verify exposures.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring the refractive index.
words.example: Winemakers use a refractometer to gauge sugar.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring colour intensity.
words.example: A colorimeter validated the paint match.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring the wavelengths of a spectrum.
words.example: The mass spectrometer identified the compound.
The calorimeter measured the reaction's enthalpy.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring high temperatures.
An instrument for controlling electric resistance.
words.example: A rheostat dimmed the theatre lights.

words.definition: An instrument for displaying electronic signals as waveforms.
words.example: The oscilloscope showed a clean sine wave.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring lung capacity.
words.example: The spirometer indicated reduced lung function.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring the intensity of radiation.
words.example: An actinometer monitored the UV lamp.

words.definition: An instrument for measuring hearing acuity.
An instrument for measuring radiation dose.

words.example: Phones use an accelerometer for step counts.
The AH meter tracked battery use.
The hydrometer floated in the syrup.
The lux meter checked office lighting.
The mass spectrometer identified the compound.
A pulse oximeter clipped to her finger.

words.definition: Measures volume changes in the body.

words.example: The marine speedometer read ten knots.
The stopwatch clicked at the finish.
The tape measure ran twenty metres.
The wind vane turned toward the sea.
